response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has 15+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and distributed systems, and demonstrates genuine technical depth in high-throughput microservices and open-source platform development. However, the role is a Principal/IC6 position explicitly centered on experimentation platforms, feature flagging, rollout automation, telemetry infrastructure, and statistical analysis — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His career has been generalist full-stack engineering rather than growth or measurement-systems work, and there is no evidence of collaboration with data science, A/B testing frameworks, or metrics pipelines. The domain gap is too large for this specific role regardless of his general engineering competence.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on engineering across TypeScript, C, C++, Java, C#, and DLang, satisfying the language breadth requirement
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency constraints
Proven platform-builder mindset through GISCollective and multiple open-source libraries (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), showing ability to create multi-consumer developer infrastructure
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience — no mention of A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, experiment assignment/targeting, or rollout-risk management anywhere in the resume
No telemetry, metrics infrastructure, or observability pipeline experience, which are foundational responsibilities of this role
No evidence of statistical analysis, data science collaboration, or growth engineering, making the experimentation quality and bias-detection requirements unmet

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background
